nodes.description: A subset of pediatric idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis cases show ANCA, ANA, or coeliac antibody positivity, supporting autoimmunity as a modifier or contributor rather than a universal primary cause.
nodes.id: Autoimmune serologic contribution to hemorrhage susceptibility

evidence.reference_title: New insights into pediatric idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis: the French RespiRare cohort.
evidence.snippet: In screened patients, initial auto-immune screening revealed positive antineutrophilic cytoplasmic antibodies (ANCA) (n = 6, 40%), antinuclear antibodies (ANA) (n = 5, 45%) and specific coeliac disease antibodies (n = 4, 28%).

evidence.reference_title: New insights into pediatric idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis: the French RespiRare cohort.
evidence.snippet: Analysis of potential contributors supports a role of auto-immunity in disease development and highlights the importance of genetic factors.

nodes.description: Diffuse alveolar hemorrhage is the core lesion of IPH and can be recurrent.

evidence.snippet: HP:0025420 | Diffuse alveolar hemorrhage | Occasional (29-5%)
repetitive episodes of diffuse alveolar haemorrhage

nodes.description: Fatigue is consistent with anemia and chronic respiratory disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012378 | Fatigue | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hemoptysis reflects alveolar hemorrhage but may be absent in children who cannot expectorate.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002105 | Hemoptysis | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.
evidence.snippet: hemoptysis (n = 61, 57%)

nodes.description: Alveolar macrophages phagocytose extravasated erythrocytes after repeated hemorrhage and accumulate hemosiderin iron. Detection of these siderophages in sputum, gastric lavage fluid, bronchoalveolar lavage fluid, or lung tissue is a diagnostic hallmark and links pulmonary blood loss to systemic iron deficiency.

evidence.snippet: Many patients develop iron deficiency anaemia secondary to deposition of haemosiderin iron in the alveoli.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.
evidence.snippet: Hemosiderin-laden macrophages were found in all of the patients.

nodes.description: Iron deficiency anemia is a cardinal manifestation of chronic pulmonary blood loss.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001891 | Iron deficiency anemia | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.

nodes.description: Pallor accompanies anemia from recurrent pulmonary blood loss.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000980 | Pallor | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Severe or relapsing disease can progress to pulmonary fibrosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002206 | Pulmonary fibrosis | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: New insights into pediatric idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis: the French RespiRare cohort.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed severe pulmonary fibrosis

nodes.description: Chest imaging commonly shows pulmonary infiltrates during hemorrhagic episodes.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002113 | Pulmonary infiltrates | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Recurrent alveolar bleeding, iron/heme deposition, and inflammatory repair injure alveolar units and can progress to restrictive physiology, respiratory failure, and pulmonary fibrosis in severe or relapsing disease.
nodes.id: Recurrent hemorrhage-driven lung injury and remodeling

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.
evidence.snippet: Death may quickly occur with acute, massive, pulmonary hemorrhage or may occur over longer periods as the result of continued respiratory insufficiency and heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into pediatric idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis: the French RespiRare cohort.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed severe pulmonary fibrosis, and another with Down syndrome died as a result of severe pulmonary hemorrhage.

nodes.description: The initiating lesion is recurrent bleeding into alveolar spaces without an identified secondary cause. Proposed environmental, allergic, autoimmune, and genetic contributors remain hypotheses rather than established causes.

evidence.snippet: Idiopathic pulmonary haemosiderosis is a rare cause of diffuse alveolar haemorrhage of unknown aetiology.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.
evidence.snippet: There are four main etiological hypotheses of IPH described in the literature, including environmental, allergic, autoimmune, and genetic hypotheses.

nodes.description: Acute massive hemorrhage or chronic respiratory insufficiency can cause respiratory failure.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002878 | Respiratory failure | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and prognosis of idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis in pediatric patients.
evidence.snippet: three died from massive pulmonary hemorrhage and respiratory failure

nodes.description: Restrictive physiology may emerge with chronic parenchymal injury or fibrosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002091 | Restrictive ventilatory defect | Occasional (29-5%)
